strengths: Proven automation and ETL pipeline delivery
BI/reporting expertise in Tableau and Power BI
Quantified impact on revenue and efficiency
weaknesses: Only 2+ years experience borderline (resume shows ~2+ years)
No explicit experience with Azure data infra
No mention of familiarity with NBA-specific data portals
improvement_bullets: Architected a production-grade ELT pipeline using Python, SQL, and dbt to model data into star schemas, ensuring robust data structures for team-wide business intelligence.
Streamlined data submission portals and reporting processes by automating workflows, reducing manual input errors and expediting insight delivery to key stakeholders.
Consulted with business analysts and cross-functional teams to translate complex business needs into automated data flows, leveraging cloud infrastructures like Snowflake and BigQuery.
Developed predictive models and performed A/B testing on marketing campaigns, driving fan engagement and revenue growth through data-informed decision-making.
Standardized organizational KPIs and data governance frameworks, facilitating cross-functional alignment and acting as a technical bridge between legal, strategic, and data teams.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Team Data Scientist opportunity with the National Basketball Association (NBA). I am interested in this opportunity because of the league's unparalleled ability to connect with a global fanbase through data-driven storytelling and business innovation. What interests me about working for the NBA is its commitment to leveraging advanced analytics to enhance fan experiences and team business operations, which inspires me because of my own passion for sports analytics and technical architecture. I believe my expertise in building scalable data pipelines and my ability to translate complex technical findings into strategic business recommendations would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by bridging the gap between raw data infrastructure and executive decision-making. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I developed automated Power BI workflows and statistical models that replaced manual reporting and identified high-value customer segments. This work resulted in a 300% increase in revenue for the targeted segment and saved 8 hours per week in operational overhead, a direct application of the efficiency and reporting improvements required for the Team Data Scientist role. Ultimately, I know that the Team Data Scientist role at the NBA would allow me to bring my proficiency in dbt and cloud data infrastructures to optimize the league's key data submission portals. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow enabled me to develop my skills in translating raw data into actionable dashboards. I analyzed 250K+ records to identify demographic clusters and visualized them in Tableau to support city-wide safety initiatives, demonstrating the exact type of stakeholder reporting required for the TMBO team. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in SQL and Python in working on predictive modeling and fan journey analysis at the NBA. I know that my technical ownership of data products and my focus on business-aligned strategy align with what it takes to succeed at the Team Data Scientist role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
